title: vicia alpestris var. hypoleuca
intro: Vicia alpestris var. hypoleuca, also known as mountain vetch, is a variant of the Vicia alpestris plant species, which is a type of annual or biennial legume native to mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of mountain vetch include its ability to support kidney function, relieve symptoms of arthritis, and help manage blood pressure. Mountain vetch can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, often in combination with other herbs, to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
